diagrams.title: Three-Schema Architecture
diagrams.description: Shows the three levels of database abstraction: External (user view), Conceptual (logical), and Internal (physical), with arrows indicating dependencies
diagrams.purpose: Understand data abstraction and independence levels in DBMS architecture

diagrams.title: Database System Environment Components
diagrams.description: Five components: Hardware, Software, Data, Procedures, Users and their interactions
diagrams.purpose: Visualize the complete database ecosystem and component relationships

diagrams.title: Schema vs Instance
diagrams.description: Comparison showing schema as empty form template and instance as filled form with actual data
elements: Arrows showing Schema defines Instance structure
Label showing Schema = structure, Instance = data
diagrams.purpose: Clarify the difference between database structure (schema) and actual data (instance)

diagrams.title: DBMS Component Architecture
diagrams.description: Internal architecture showing how DBMS components work together
diagrams.purpose: Understand how DBMS internally processes queries and manages data
